Realistic Evaluation

Book overview and chapter-level study-note index for Realistic Evaluation.

Book notes

Realistic Evaluation

Chapter-level study notes on Realistic Evaluation (Pawson and Tilley 1997). These notes are generated from source-linked MCP research-library records and are intended for study, comparison, and later refinement.

10 chapter notes 1997 Source status: needs_review MCP evaluation-texts

Synthesis

Core Concepts

Recurring ideas and distinctions used across these chapter notes.

Map

Chapter Map

Source chapters, page ranges, and reading routes.

Practice

Practice Implications

Practical evaluation design habits drawn from the notes.

Source

1997MCP evaluation-textsStatus: needs_reviewBook overview

This index summarises generated chapter-level study notes for Ray Pawson and Nick Tilley’s Realistic Evaluation (Pawson and Tilley 1997). The generated pages use selected MCP research-library retrieval records and compact evidence summaries.

Core Argument

Realistic evaluation explains programs through CMO configurations: programs trigger mechanisms in contexts to produce outcome patterns, and evaluation tests and refines those explanations for policy and practice.

Chapter Notes

Orientation

  • Introduction - source pages 10-18

Critique of Existing Evaluation Traditions

  • Chapter 1: A History of Evaluation in 28 1/2 Pages - source pages 18-46
  • Chapter 2: Out with the Old: Weaknesses in Experimental Evaluation - source pages 47-71

Realist Foundation

  • Chapter 3: In with the New: Introducing Scientific Realism - source pages 72-100

Design and Cumulation

  • Chapter 4: How to Design a Realistic Evaluation - source pages 100-130
  • Chapter 5: How to Make Evaluations Cumulate - source pages 132-170

Data Construction

  • Chapter 6: How to Construct Realistic Data: Utilizing Stakeholders’ Knowledge - source pages 171-199

Worked Application

  • Chapter 7: No Smoking Without Firing Mechanisms: A ‘Realistic’ Consultation - source pages 200-216

Policy and Practice

  • Chapter 8: Evaluation, Policy and Practice: Realizing the Potential - source pages 217-230

Synthesis

  • Chapter 9: The New Rules of Realistic Evaluation - source pages 231-236

Legacy Extracts

  • Reader’s Guide to Context, Mechanism, and Outcome
  • Scientific Realism and CMO Hypotheses
  • The New Rules and CMO Configurations
